phases.name: Forge Source of Truth Realignment
phases.objective: Make the Forge UI the accurate product map for Zo-Qore and Victor by unifying roadmap, mindmap, and verification evidence around the full governed backlog.
nestedPrioritySummary: P0: Builder roadmap and mindmap reflect the full product plan instead of UI-only slices.
P0: Recursive priorities are visible to arbitrary depth in roadmap tasks.
P1: Visual confirmation path exists so roadmap and mindmap changes can be substantiated quickly.

tasks.title: Promote builder-console to the canonical product roadmap
tasks.description: Fold Victor execution, memory, packaging, quarantine, and EvolveAI workstreams into the Builder project so the UI shows the actual product plan instead of an incomplete implementation slice.
acceptance: Builder roadmap includes the Victor phases that materially define the product.
Packaging, memory, and EvolveAI are represented as first-class workstreams.
Builder data becomes the visible source of truth for the product map.

children.title: Import Victor roadmap tracks into builder-console planning data
Add packaging and EvolveAI phases missing from the visible roadmap
Mark builder-console as the visualization source of truth for the product

tasks.title: Render recursive nested priorities inside roadmap tasks
tasks.description: Expose multi-level execution priorities in the roadmap so each step can fan out into sub-priorities without flattening critical sequencing.
acceptance: Tasks can display nested priority trees to arbitrary depth.
Priority labels remain readable on desktop and mobile.
Nested work can distinguish critical path, enabling work, and validation work.

children.title: Add recursive task rendering in the roadmap view
Add badges and hierarchy styling for priority depth
Verify the rendering handles deep trees without collapsing readability

tasks.title: Replace mock mindmap behavior with real Builder planning data
tasks.description: Stop falling back to demo/legacy mindmap payloads when the Forge UI can resolve the actual planning files and derived constellation map.
acceptance: Mindmap views load builder-console constellation and cluster data by default.
Mock data is only used for explicit empty-state handling, not normal operation.
Roadmap and mindmap read from the same canonical planning project.

children.title: Resolve builder-console as the default project when none is selected
Fetch clusters and constellation from planning APIs instead of legacy endpoints
Keep honest empty states when no real data exists

tasks.title: Substantiate roadmap and mindmap changes with live preview evidence
tasks.description: Provide a direct verification path so plan changes can be visually confirmed instead of assumed from JSON edits alone.
acceptance: Local preview is exercised after roadmap/mindmap changes.
Visual confirmation covers both roadmap and mindmap surfaces.
The session leaves durable evidence describing what was verified.

children.title: Run the UI locally against the updated planning data
Capture screenshots for roadmap and mindmap confirmation
Record the verification outcome in workspace memory

phases.name: Comms Tab Prompt Automation
phases.objective: Automate prompt-building inside the comms tab so the user experience collapses to standard chat input/output plus a compact operations display showing prompt construction in real time.

tasks.title: Automate comms-tab prompt construction
tasks.description: Move prompt-building components behind an automated pipeline so the visible interface becomes standard chat input/output with a small operations panel that streams prompt-construction steps as they happen.
acceptance: Prompt-building logic is automated instead of manually assembled through multiple visible components.
The comms tab primary surface is reduced to normal chat input/output.
A compact operations display shows prompt construction/provenance in real time.
The operations display is observable without overwhelming the main conversation flow.
The resulting architecture is ready for Victor-mediated automation under Forge governance.

tasks.title: Build backend prompt-construction pipeline
tasks.description: Implement the backend path that assembles prompt inputs, applies governed construction steps, and emits structured prompt-construction events for the comms tab operations display.
acceptance: Prompt inputs are assembled by a backend pipeline instead of manual UI composition.
The pipeline emits structured prompt-construction events or provenance records.
The backend path is ready to feed a compact operations display without coupling to the main chat surface.
The design remains compatible with Victor-mediated governed automation.

tasks.title: Build prompt-construction operations display
tasks.description: Implement the compact operations panel that streams prompt-construction steps, provenance, and system state without overwhelming the main chat input/output surface.
acceptance: The operations display shows prompt-construction steps in real time.
Provenance or system-state details are visible without taking over the primary chat surface.
The display consumes structured backend prompt-construction events rather than reconstructing state in the UI.
The resulting interface stays aligned with Victor-mediated governed automation.

tasks.title: Reduce comms tab to standard chat I/O
tasks.description: Refactor the comms tab so the visible primary interface becomes standard chat input/output, with prompt construction moved behind the governed backend pipeline and exposed only through the compact operations display.
acceptance: The primary comms-tab surface is standard chat input/output.
Prompt-building controls are removed from the main visible surface.
The chat surface consumes the governed backend prompt-construction pipeline instead of assembling prompts directly in the UI.
Prompt-construction visibility is delegated to the compact operations display.
The resulting interface stays aligned with Victor-mediated governed automation.

tasks.title: Implement automation audit logging
tasks.description: Add append-only automation run and action audit records with run IDs, governance outcomes, before/after mutation snapshots, actor labels, and a queryable API for overnight review.
acceptance: Automation runs produce run-started, action, and run-completed audit entries.
Audit entries capture governance results and before/after state for task mutations.
Victor exposes a query path for recent automation audit history.

phases.name: Heartbeat Autonomy Foundation
phases.objective: Extend Victor from supervised phase-by-phase execution into sustainable governed heartbeat operation with explicit separation between project build progress and non-project self-reflection.

tasks.title: Separate non-project reflection from Builder artifacts
tasks.description: Move heartbeat self-reflection into a system-level memory/audit path so Forge only carries project-relevant planning notes and build progress.
acceptance: Heartbeat self-reflection can be written without attaching to a Builder phase or cluster.
Forge continues to receive only project-relevant planning notes and build-progress state.
Automation activity and build progress remain queryable as separate surfaces.

tasks.title: Teach heartbeat to select the next governed slice after phase closure
tasks.description: Allow heartbeat to recognize completed governed phases and either select an existing pending phase or stop cleanly with an explicit recommendation for the next slice.
acceptance: Heartbeat does not stall or fabricate work when the active governed phase is complete.
Heartbeat can identify the next eligible governed phase or produce a governed recommendation instead of looping on a finished phase.
The transition decision is visible in automation audit records.

tasks.title: Prepare unattended 30m heartbeat operation with safe stop conditions
tasks.description: Harden the 30-minute heartbeat path so Victor can run unattended under bounded action budgets, cooldown rules, and explicit stop/escalation conditions.
acceptance: Unattended 30m heartbeat execution respects focus-window, cooldown, and action-budget policy.
Repeated blocks or missing eligible work stop the unattended loop cleanly instead of churning.
Audit output is sufficient to explain unattended heartbeat behavior after the fact.

phases.name: 30m Heartbeat Dry-Run Soak Test
phases.objective: Run a bounded unattended 30m dry-run heartbeat against real governed work and verify safety signals before unattended execution is allowed.

tasks.title: Run unattended 30m dry-run heartbeat soak
tasks.description: Run the real unattended heartbeat loop at 30m baseline in dry-run mode with explicit tick and runtime limits.
acceptance: Heartbeat starts cleanly, remains bounded, and emits audit-backed tick evidence without writes.

tasks.title: Review dry-run soak audit signals
tasks.description: Inspect run state, selected tasks, stop conditions, and blocked reasons from the soak output.
acceptance: The soak produces enough evidence to judge whether unattended execution is safe to promote.

tasks.title: Define unattended execute-mode promotion gate
tasks.description: Turn soak evidence into explicit promotion criteria for unattended write-capable heartbeat operation.
acceptance: Promotion criteria are explicit, auditable, and stricter than the dry-run soak baseline.

phases.name: Forecast and Planning Operations
phases.objective: Turn the new Victor and Builder planning artifacts into Builder-managed operational work so unattended execution has a governed next queue.

tasks.title: Forecast remaining Builder delivery work
tasks.description: Translate the current Builder forecast into Builder-managed operational tasks and dependency-aware planning state.
acceptance: Builder has an explicit next-step forecast instead of relying on narrative summaries.

tasks.title: Project Victor autonomy dependency impact into Builder surfaces
tasks.description: Expose how Victor promotion state affects Builder delivery readiness without collapsing the Victor and Builder views together.
acceptance: Builder surfaces reflect Victor dependency impact only where it materially affects delivery.

tasks.title: Queue next governed slices across Victor and Builder
tasks.description: Keep the next governed work items explicit across both project tracks so unattended execution advances known priorities instead of fabricating work.
acceptance: The next governed slices for Victor and Builder are Builder-visible and ordered.

tasks.title: Create fresh Victor and Forge UI shell
tasks.description: Create a new UI surface for Victor and Builder views, preserving the legacy UI while transferring forward only the data views and interaction patterns that prove useful.
acceptance: A new UI surface exists without deleting or replacing the current legacy UI.
Victor and Builder remain distinct switchable views in the new shell.
Only proven data views and interaction patterns are transferred from the old UI.
The new UI is grounded in Builder project state instead of imaginary placeholder surfaces.

tasks.title: Build Victor shell hero and verdict surface
tasks.description: Turn the fresh shell foundation into a real Victor surface with the hybrid portrait and verdict altar bound to live promotion and heartbeat state.
acceptance: Victor shell renders the hybrid portrait plus verdict altar using live data.
Promotion verdict, heartbeat state, and unmet criteria are legible without placeholder language.
The hero moment remains restrained outside the central Victor surface.

tasks.title: Build Forge shell command surface
tasks.description: Add the austere Builder view to the fresh shell with live progress, forecast, dependency impact, and governed queue information.
acceptance: Forge shell renders real build progress and governed queue state.
Victor dependency impact only appears when it materially affects Builder delivery.
Historical blocked runs are not mislabeled as current blockers.

tasks.title: Audit and substantiate fresh shell truthfulness
tasks.description: Review the new shell against live data, the web design guidelines skill, and responsive behavior so the UI can be trusted before Victor expands automation on it.
acceptance: The shell is reviewed against the Vercel web guidelines.
Mobile and desktop responsive behavior are checked on the new route.
Observed, inferred, and unverified states are distinguishable in the shell where relevant.

phases.name: Quarantine Visibility UI
phases.objective: Extend Forge UI to surface quarantine state, scan results, and promotion/rejection history for human inspection.

tasks.title: Extend HubPayload with quarantine data
tasks.description: Add quarantine stats (counts by state, verdict distribution, last fetch time), recent items summary, pending review count, and exploration status to HubPayload type and hub.ts builder. Null-safe when quarantine is disabled or store is empty.
acceptance: HubPayload includes quarantine field with stats, recentItems, pendingReview, explorationStatus
buildHubPayload reads from quarantine store when available
Null-safe fallback when quarantine is disabled or store missing
TypeScript compilation succeeds

tasks.title: Build quarantine dashboard component
tasks.description: Summary view in Forge Victor surface: total items by state (quarantined/provisional/rejected), scan verdict distribution (clean/suspicious/hostile), last fetch timestamp, exploration tick count and status, alert banner when suspicious items await human review.
acceptance: Dashboard renders live quarantine stats from HubPayload
State counts (quarantined/provisional/rejected) are visible
Verdict distribution (clean/suspicious/hostile) is visible
Alert banner appears when pendingReview > 0
Exploration status (idle/active/paused/disabled) is legible
Dashboard degrades gracefully when quarantine is disabled

tasks.title: Build quarantine feed component
tasks.description: Scrollable list of quarantined items, newest first. Each item shows: title, author, source, timestamp, scan verdict badge (green/yellow/red), scan category chips, state badge, content preview (first 200 chars expandable), and Promote/Reject action buttons for human override.
acceptance: Feed renders items newest-first with all required fields
Verdict badges use color coding: green (clean), yellow (suspicious), red (hostile)
Promote and Reject buttons call quarantine store endpoints
State updates reflect immediately in the feed after action
Content preview is truncated with expand capability

tasks.title: Build scan detail inspector
tasks.description: Expandable or modal detail view for individual quarantine items: full sanitized content, raw content diff showing what was stripped, all scan categories with match/no-match and evidence strings, cumulative threat score breakdown, provenance (source URL, post ID, author, fetch time).
acceptance: Inspector shows full sanitized content
Raw vs sanitized diff is visible (stripped elements highlighted)
All 10 scan categories show match status and evidence
Threat score breakdown is legible
Provenance information is complete (source, author, timestamps)

tasks.title: Build quarantine audit log viewer
tasks.description: Filterable table of quarantine ledger entries. Filter by: event type (fetch/scan/gate-promote/gate-reject/manual-promote/manual-reject/prune), verdict, date range, source. JSONL export capability.
acceptance: Log table renders ledger entries with all fields
Filters work for event type, verdict, and date range
Export produces valid JSONL file
Table handles large ledger files without performance degradation
Empty state shown when no ledger entries exist

phases.name: Victor Lifecycle and Forecast Foundation
phases.objective: Model Victor as a separate governed project inside Builder and define the forecasting signals for remaining autonomy work.

tasks.title: Model Victor project lifecycle from concept to completion
tasks.description: Define the stages Builder should use to track Victor work without collapsing it into Builder infrastructure work.
acceptance: Victor lifecycle stages are explicit and separate from Forge infrastructure stages.

tasks.title: Define Victor remaining-effort forecast signals
tasks.description: Specify confidence, blockers, dependencies, and effort-band inputs for forecasting Victor progress.
acceptance: Victor forecast inputs are evidence-backed and separate from generic automation activity.

tasks.title: Record Builder dependencies that gate Victor autonomy promotion
tasks.description: Capture which Builder capabilities must mature before Victor can safely escalate autonomy.
acceptance: Victor dependencies on Builder infrastructure are explicit and governable.

phases.name: Unattended Execute Promotion Review
phases.objective: Define the execute-mode promotion bar for Victor and tie unattended operation to explicit evidence, fallback, and revocation rules.

tasks.title: Define Victor unattended execute-mode promotion criteria
tasks.description: Turn the current dry-run soak and dependency gates into explicit promotion criteria for unattended write-capable heartbeat operation.
acceptance: Execute-mode promotion criteria are explicit, governed, and stricter than dry-run soak success alone.

tasks.title: Review 30m dry-run soak evidence against promotion gate
tasks.description: Compare the unattended 30m dry-run audit trail against the execute-mode promotion criteria before any autonomy increase.
acceptance: Dry-run soak evidence is reviewed against the gate and any missing proof is explicit.

tasks.title: Record unattended execute fallback and revocation triggers
tasks.description: Capture the kill conditions, fallback rules, and revocation criteria that should force Victor out of unattended execute mode.
acceptance: Execute-mode fallback and revocation triggers are explicit and Builder-visible.

phases.name: Bounded Unattended Execute Pilot
phases.objective: Run the first bounded unattended execute window, review its evidence, and decide whether Victor has earned any wider autonomy.

tasks.title: Run bounded unattended 30m execute pilot
tasks.description: Run Victor in unattended execute mode at the 30-minute baseline with explicit kill-switch, tick, and runtime limits.
acceptance: Unattended execute pilot runs within the bounded action budget and leaves a reviewable verification packet.

tasks.title: Review unattended execute verification packet
tasks.description: Inspect the verification-grade report after the unattended execute pilot and classify any drift, block, or revocation signals.
acceptance: Observed, inferred, and unverified claims are reviewed before any autonomy expansion decision.

tasks.title: Define post-pilot autonomy expansion rule
tasks.description: Decide what evidence would be required before Victor gains broader unattended scope, faster cadence, or wider project coverage.
acceptance: Autonomy expansion remains evidence-gated and does not self-authorize from a single successful pilot.

phases.name: Unified Governance Bridge
phases.objective: Bridge Victor's quarantine pipeline and execute-pilot infrastructure into a unified governance layer that governs all automated actions under a single consistent policy framework.

tasks.title: Unified Governance Policy Framework
tasks.description: Define a single policy schema that can govern both content admission and action execution with trust tiers, confidence thresholds, and required verification steps.
acceptance: Policy schema supports both content trust tiers and action risk levels
Policy defines confidence thresholds per tier/level combination
Policy defines required scan categories for content vs required verification steps for actions
Policy is versioned and auditable

tasks.title: Governance Decision Engine
tasks.description: Build a unified decision engine that evaluates both content admission requests and action execution requests against the policy framework.
acceptance: Single evaluate() method handles both ContentAdmissionRequest and ActionExecutionRequest
Returns consistent GovernanceDecision type with verdict, confidence, and reasoning
Integrates with existing quarantine-gate.ts for content and execute-pilot.ts for actions
Decision audit trail written to unified ledger

tasks.title: Unified Audit Ledger
tasks.description: Extend the quarantine audit ledger to record all governance decisions—content and actions—in a single queryable history.
acceptance: Ledger entries support both content events and action events
Query API filters by event type, verdict, project, timestamp range
Unified export produces coherent timeline of all governed activity
Retention policies apply consistently across event types

tasks.title: Cross-Domain Policy Enforcement
tasks.description: Ensure policy constraints flow correctly between content and action domains—e.g., external-untrusted content triggers higher scrutiny on derived actions.
acceptance: Content trust tier influences action risk assessment
Action audit records reference source content IDs where applicable
Policy violations in one domain can trigger review requirements in the other
No circular dependencies between content and action evaluation

tasks.title: Unified Governance CLI
tasks.description: Create a command-line interface for governance operations: policy inspection, manual ledger queries, emergency revocation, and audit export.
acceptance: CLI supports inspect-policy, query-ledger, revoke-run, export-audit commands
Commands accept project-id and time range filters
Output formats: human-readable, JSON, JSONL
Exit codes indicate success (0), policy violation found (2), or error (1)

phases.name: Execute Mode Governance
phases.objective: Establish tiered governance, monitoring, and oversight systems for Victor execute-mode operation across all autonomy levels.

tasks.title: Tiered Governance Structure
tasks.description: Define governance rules for Tier 1/2/3 execute modes. Create authorization flows (user explicit, evidence-gated, automated). Document escalation procedures for each tier. Define tier promotion/demotion criteria.
acceptance: Each tier has explicit authorization requirements
Governance flows are documented and testable
Escalation procedures are defined for each tier

tasks.title: Monitoring Dashboard
tasks.description: Build real-time execute-mode status display with revocation trigger visibility, tier progress tracking, governance violation alerts, and observation window countdown.
acceptance: Dashboard shows live execute-mode status
Revocation triggers visible in real-time
Tier progress tracked and displayed

tasks.title: Audit Trail System
tasks.description: Create searchable, exportable audit trail with evidence linking for all actions, verification packet generation, and historical query interface.
acceptance: All actions logged with evidence links
Audit trail searchable and exportable
Verification packet generation automated

phases.name: Moltbook Quarantine Pipeline
phases.objective: Implement quarantine-first pipeline for external content ingestion (Moltbook), with sanitization, adversarial scanning, governance gating, and audit logging to safely explore external agent discourse during heartbeat idle periods.

tasks.title: Add quarantine governance types
tasks.description: Add 'quarantined' GovernanceState, SourceTrustTier, SourceTrustMetadata, ScanVerdict, ScanDetail, ScanCategory types. Update policy JSON with source trust tiers and quarantine settings.
acceptance: New types compile
Policy loads with quarantine config
Existing tests unbroken

tasks.title: Build quarantine sanitizer
tasks.description: Implement quarantine-sanitize.ts: HTML/script tag stripping, base64 block decoding, unicode normalization, control character removal, length truncation, encoded segment flagging.
acceptance: Sanitizer strips all known encoding vectors
Unit tests cover each sanitization category
Raw and sanitized content both preserved

tasks.title: Build adversarial scan engine
tasks.description: Implement quarantine-scan.ts with 4-layer scanner: literal patterns (50+), structural heuristics, encoding detection, similarity/flood detection. Produces ScanVerdict and ScanDetail[].
acceptance: Scanner detects all categories
Unit tests cover clean, suspicious, hostile paths
False positive rate acceptable on benign content

tasks.title: Build quarantine governance gate
tasks.description: Implement quarantine-governance.ts: route scan verdicts to auto-reject (hostile), quarantine (suspicious), or quarantine-with-auto-promote (clean). Apply source trust tier confidence caps.
acceptance: Hostile auto-rejects
Suspicious quarantines for review
Clean quarantines with cooldown promotion
Confidence never exceeds tier cap

tasks.title: Build quarantine store
tasks.description: Implement quarantine-store.ts with SQLite backend. CRUD operations, promotion/rejection, stats, pruning, max-items enforcement.
acceptance: Store persists across restarts
FIFO eviction works
Promotion and rejection state transitions correct

tasks.title: Build quarantine audit ledger
tasks.description: Implement quarantine-ledger.ts: append-only JSONL, queryable by event type and date range. Every pipeline stage logs to ledger.
acceptance: Every pipeline stage logs to ledger
Ledger is append-only and survives restarts

tasks.title: Build Moltbook fetch client
tasks.description: Implement moltbook-fetch.ts: read-only GET client with rate limiting (1 batch/tick, max 10 posts), content truncation (4000 chars), batch size limits. No write endpoints.
acceptance: Client fetches posts
No write endpoints called
Rate limits enforced
Content truncated

tasks.title: Wire heartbeat idle exploration
tasks.description: Add explore mode to heartbeat: when build queue empty, run Moltbook quarantine pipeline instead of parking. Respect maxConsecutiveExplore and cooldown.
acceptance: Exploration runs only when build queue empty
Heartbeat parks after maxConsecutiveExplore
Exploration ticks are audited

phases.name: Packaging Plane: Unified Ingress
phases.objective: Package the boring but necessary automation infrastructure so channels, webhooks, cron, email ingress, and governed event envelopes stop feeling like custom glue.
nestedPrioritySummary: P0: Every ingress path emits a common governed event envelope.
P1: Operator-visible ingress registry shows enabled channels and trust state.
P2: Add adapters only after the envelope contract is stable.

tasks.title: Define a unified ingress event contract
tasks.description: Normalize chat, webhook, cron, Gmail Pub/Sub, and future channel triggers into one envelope with provenance, actor identity, requested capability, and governance context.
acceptance: All ingress paths map to a shared event envelope.
Envelope fields include provenance, actor identity, and requested capability.
Governance can evaluate ingress uniformly before execution dispatch.

children.title: Define core envelope schema and actor identity rules
Map cron, webhook, channel, and Gmail ingress into the schema
Document which ingress classes are allowed to self-trigger execution

tasks.title: Build an operator-visible ingress registry
tasks.description: Expose which channels exist, their auth posture, their current health, and the event classes they may emit so packaging does not become hidden coupling.
acceptance: Ingress adapters appear in a single registry surface.
Registry entries show auth state, health, and emitted event classes.
Operators can tell which ingress paths are enabled without reading code.

children.title: List registered ingress adapters and trust state
Show health and last-seen event details for each adapter

tasks.title: Add deterministic replay for ingress events
tasks.description: Make ingress debuggable by allowing recorded envelopes to be replayed through governance and dispatch without touching live channels.
acceptance: Recorded ingress events can be replayed deterministically.
Replay mode preserves governance decisions and audit output.
Operators can debug channel behavior without fabricating new events.

children.title: Persist replayable ingress envelopes with redacted secrets
Run replay through the same governance path as live events

phases.name: Packaging Plane: Execution Gateway and Adapters
phases.objective: Package browser, exec, web, provider, service, and plugin actions behind one bounded execution surface so Victor stops feeling like a pile of handwritten adapters.
nestedPrioritySummary: P0: One execution gateway owns approvals, budgets, and audit hooks.
P1: Adapters/plugins conform to a single capability contract.
P1: Execution health and auth boundary failures are visible to operators.

tasks.title: Consolidate action dispatch behind a governed execution gateway
tasks.description: Route browser, shell, HTTP, and provider actions through a single gateway that enforces budgets, auth context, audit logging, and fallbacks consistently.
acceptance: Action dispatch passes through one execution gateway.
Gateway enforces budgets, auth context, and audit hooks uniformly.
Execution behavior is observable without tracing each adapter separately.

children.title: Define capability registry and dispatch contract
Centralize budgets, approvals, and failure handling
Expose per-capability metrics and recent audit outcomes

tasks.title: Define plugin, provider, route, and service adapter contracts
tasks.description: Make adapters swappable by specifying the contract for capabilities, inputs, auth, result shape, error semantics, and governance metadata.
acceptance: Adapters share one capability contract.
Result and error semantics are normalized across adapter types.
Governance metadata is emitted by every adapter, not only core ones.

children.title: Specify adapter interface for browser, exec, web, service, provider, plugin
Normalize auth and result metadata across adapters

tasks.title: Add adapter verification and boundary tests
tasks.description: Make packaging trustworthy by validating adapters for auth leakage, timeout behavior, retry semantics, and audit completeness before promotion.
acceptance: Adapters have verification coverage for auth, timeout, and retry boundaries.
Boundary failures are visible before runtime promotion.
Verification output is stored with the adapter or gateway artifacts.

children.title: Test auth boundary handling and redaction behavior
Test timeout, retry, and partial-failure semantics
Require verification packets before adapter promotion

phases.name: Memory Governance Core Hardening
phases.objective: Formalize the epistemic, policy, and audit primitives that make Victor's memory system inspectable, phase-gated, and safe to promote beyond structural GraphRAG capability.

tasks.title: Add artifact-level governance primitives to memory records
tasks.description: Extend Victor memory records with governance state, epistemic type, confidence profile, policy version, and provenance completeness so stored memory distinguishes observation, inference, synthesis, and contested material.
acceptance: Memory records carry explicit governance state and epistemic typing.
Confidence and provenance-completeness metadata are inspectable at the artifact level.
Stored memory can distinguish source-grounded observation from derived synthesis.

children.title: Extend artifact schema with epistemic type, governance state, provenance completeness
Expose those fields in retrieval bundles and audit output
Verify contested and inferred material stays distinguishable in UI and ledger

tasks.title: Externalize declarative memory promotion and recall policy
tasks.description: Create a dedicated memory policy artifact that defines promotion thresholds, recall modes, contradiction rules, and stale-cache handling instead of leaving those semantics embedded in code paths.
acceptance: Memory promotion and recall thresholds live in a declarative policy file.
Contradiction and stale-cache rules are inspectable without reading implementation code.
Policy versioning is wired into memory governance decisions.

children.title: Move promotion, contradiction, stale-cache rules into declarative policy artifacts
Version policy decisions and surface the active revision to operators

tasks.title: Add deterministic policy interception across memory lifecycle phases
tasks.description: Introduce deterministic triage and per-phase policy checkpoints for encode, recall, persistence, and crystallization so memory operations are governed at every phase instead of only at write time.
acceptance: Memory lifecycle phases expose policy checkpoints with phase-specific context.
Policy interception remains deterministic and auditable instead of LLM-classified.
Rejected or audit-only decisions are surfaced before state mutation.

children.title: Create deterministic checkpoints for encode, recall, persistence, crystallization
Block or downgrade before mutation when policy fails

tasks.title: Implement strict, audit, and permissive memory policy modes
tasks.description: Support enforcement modes for memory governance so operators can run fail-closed in production, audit in tuning windows, and permissive in controlled development without drifting policy semantics.
acceptance: Memory policy supports strict, audit, and permissive modes.
Audit mode logs what would have been blocked without mutating behavior.
Mode selection is configurable globally and per phase.

children.title: Implement strict, audit, and permissive evaluation branches
Expose mode selection in the operator surface without drifting semantics

tasks.title: Add dedicated memory governance ledger events
tasks.description: Record promotion approvals, rejections, contested writes, recall downgrades, and stale-memory blocks as first-class governance events so Victor can substantiate memory behavior, not just execution behavior.
acceptance: Memory-governance decisions emit dedicated ledger events.
Ledger entries distinguish promotions, demotions, contested writes, and stale recall blocks.
Memory-governance history is queryable alongside existing audit trails.

children.title: Emit dedicated ledger events for promotion, demotion, stale recall, contested writes
Make the history queryable alongside existing execution audits

tasks.title: Split retrieval orchestration into explicit governed modules
tasks.description: Refactor retrieval into legible stages for query intent, candidate gathering, graph expansion, arbitration, and bundle assembly so governance and evidence handling are traceable instead of buried in one module.
acceptance: Retrieval orchestration stages are separated into explicit modules.
Each retrieval stage exposes its contribution to the final grounded bundle.
Governance can inspect where recall was strengthened, downgraded, or blocked.

children.title: Split query intent, candidate gather, graph expansion, arbitration, bundle assembly
Annotate each stage with why recall was strengthened, downgraded, or blocked

nestedPrioritySummary: P0: Governance primitives and policy externalization.
P1: Lifecycle interceptors, modes, and ledger events.
P1: Retrieval decomposition for audit legibility.

children.title: Extend artifact schema with epistemic type, governance state, provenance completeness
Expose those fields in retrieval bundles and audit output
Verify contested and inferred material stays distinguishable in UI and ledger
Move promotion, contradiction, stale-cache rules into declarative policy artifacts
Version policy decisions and surface the active revision to operators
Create deterministic checkpoints for encode, recall, persistence, crystallization
Block or downgrade before mutation when policy fails
Implement strict, audit, and permissive evaluation branches
Expose mode selection in the operator surface without drifting semantics
Emit dedicated ledger events for promotion, demotion, stale recall, contested writes
Make the history queryable alongside existing execution audits
Split query intent, candidate gather, graph expansion, arbitration, bundle assembly
Annotate each stage with why recall was strengthened, downgraded, or blocked

phases.name: UOR Content-Addressed Identity Migration
phases.objective: Replace ad hoc Victor memory identity with deterministic UOR addressing so memory writes, lookups, deduplication, and supersession lineage are cryptographically and semantically stable.

tasks.title: Introduce canonical UOR address type and BLAKE3 content addressing
tasks.description: Add deterministic content-addressed identity primitives so Victor memory artifacts can derive stable UOR addresses from canonical bytes instead of relying on UUIDs and duplicate hash pipelines.
acceptance: Victor memory supports a canonical UOR address type.
Content-addressed identity uses the designated BLAKE3 pipeline.
Existing SHA-based integrity paths remain backward-compatible during migration.

tasks.title: Rewire memory, tier, and graph structures onto UOR identity
tasks.description: Apply UOR identity across documents, chunks, semantic nodes, edges, tier maps, and processor snapshots so the memory stack uses one stable identity model instead of parallel keys.
acceptance: Core memory and tier structures key on UOR identity.
Graph and snapshot layers preserve referential integrity under the new address model.
Identity collisions fail closed instead of silently overwriting artifacts.

tasks.title: Add exact-address lookup for crystallized memory
tasks.description: Introduce O(1) address lookup for stable L3 memory so saturated artifacts can exit the expensive similarity-search path when exact identity is known.
acceptance: Crystallized memory supports direct address lookup.
Retrieval can short-circuit on exact L3 address matches.
Exact-match recall remains auditable and backward-compatible.

tasks.title: Run governed migration from current provenance to UOR-backed identity
tasks.description: Upgrade existing memory artifacts into the UOR identity model with explicit audits, rollback affordances, and conflict reporting so the migration itself is governed and reversible.
acceptance: Existing memory artifacts can be migrated into the UOR identity model.
Migration writes an auditable report of converted, skipped, and contested artifacts.
Rollback path exists if identity conflicts or provenance gaps are discovered.

nestedPrioritySummary: P1: Build the long-term memory substrate after governance is trustworthy.
P2: Sequence temporal, reliability, and operator ergonomics after core provenance work.

Split query intent, candidate gather, graph expansion, arbitration, bundle assembly
Annotate each stage with why recall was strengthened, downgraded, or blocked

phases.name: Thermodynamic Decay Foundation
phases.objective: Replace fixed memory decay heuristics with the designed saturation and temperature model so decay, promotion, and retrieval weight are grounded in resolvedness instead of age alone.

tasks.title: Replace fixed decay factor with saturation and temperature state
tasks.description: Refactor memory records so unresolvedness is represented through saturation and derived context temperature instead of a single opaque decay factor.
acceptance: Memory records store saturation instead of a fixed decay factor.
Context temperature and effective lambda are derived from saturation.
Existing callers can inspect thermodynamic state without reconstructing it externally.

tasks.title: Implement thermodynamic decay and saturation boost functions
tasks.description: Add temperature, effective lambda, decay, and saturation-boost primitives so memory cooling and reheating are deterministic and testable.
acceptance: Decay functions use effective lambda derived from saturation.
Saturated memories can reach zero-decay ground state.
Access-driven saturation boosts are deterministic and bounded.

tasks.title: Route crystallized memory by saturation threshold
tasks.description: Bind tier routing to saturation so highly resolved memories can crystallize into the permanent layer based on explicit thermodynamic criteria instead of only score heuristics.
acceptance: Tier routing can promote highly saturated memory to crystallized storage.
Thermodynamic thresholds are explicit and inspectable.
Routing remains compatible with existing score-based heuristics during transition.

tasks.title: Substantiate self-optimization behavior with governed tests
tasks.description: Add explicit tests and benchmarks proving that access can cool memory, reduce decay, and improve routing so thermodynamic claims are substantiated instead of rhetorical.
acceptance: Validation covers zero-decay saturation, partial-saturation decay, and routing thresholds.
Evidence shows repeated access can improve retention characteristics.
Thermodynamic behavior is queryable in test output and audit evidence.

nestedPrioritySummary: P1: Build the long-term memory substrate after governance is trustworthy.
P2: Sequence temporal, reliability, and operator ergonomics after core provenance work.

Annotate each stage with why recall was strengthened, downgraded, or blocked

phases.name: Weighted Pinning and Associative Promotion
phases.objective: Implement the designed reinforcement loop where verified use, cross-reference, conflict, and session co-capture can pin or unpin memory and drive legitimate crystallization.

tasks.title: Add weighted pinning hierarchy for access, corroboration, and verification
tasks.description: Implement differentiated pinning events so cryptographic verification, corroboration, cross-reference, and ordinary access do not all distort saturation equally.
acceptance: Pinning events carry differentiated weights.
Verification pins memory faster than ordinary access.
Weighting remains bounded and testable.

tasks.title: Implement entropy injection for contested or conflicting memory
tasks.description: Allow disputes and contradictions to unpin memory so contested material heats up, decays faster, and stops masquerading as stable knowledge.
acceptance: Conflict paths can reduce saturation deterministically.
Entropy injection is bounded and auditable.
Contested memory loses stability without destructive deletion.

tasks.title: Add session co-capture linking and peer pinning
tasks.description: Link memories encoded within the same governed session and let those relationships contribute medium-strength pinning so coherent memory clusters can form without manual authoring.
acceptance: New session memories create associative links to valid peers.
Cross-reference pinning affects linked memories deterministically.
Session boundaries remain explicit so unrelated runs do not smear together.

tasks.title: Promote L2 memory to crystallized storage on saturation threshold
tasks.description: Turn saturation crossing into a real L2 to L3 promotion path so the memory system can earn O(1) lookup and permanence through repeated grounded evidence.
acceptance: Saturation-driven promotion moves eligible memory from L2 to L3.
Promotion is explicit and testable instead of implicit routing drift.
Promoted memory becomes available to exact-address lookup.

nestedPrioritySummary: P1: Build the long-term memory substrate after governance is trustworthy.
P2: Sequence temporal, reliability, and operator ergonomics after core provenance work.

phases.name: Zero-Trust Crystallization and Provenance
phases.objective: Move crystallization from optimistic auto-promotion to explicit approval and provenance-aware trust so Victor only hardens memory that has earned it.

tasks.title: Make approval-required crystallization the default policy
tasks.description: Add explicit crystallization policy controls so Victor can require approval before promoting high-saturation memory into permanent storage.
acceptance: Crystallization policy supports approval-required and auto modes.
Default behavior is zero-trust rather than auto-promote.
Policy choice is inspectable in processor configuration and governance output.

tasks.title: Implement explicit approval flow for pending crystallization
tasks.description: Require an explicit approval path for high-saturation memories so promotion to L3 can be reviewed and substantiated instead of silently inferred.
acceptance: High-saturation L2 memory can remain pending until approved.
Approval promotes only eligible memory above the crystallization threshold.
Rejected or missing approval leaves memory in a non-crystallized state.

tasks.title: Add source trust levels to initial saturation and provenance
tasks.description: Map source provenance into trust levels so unverified, user-reviewed, and verified inputs do not all enter the system with identical initial epistemic weight.
acceptance: Input metadata captures explicit source trust level.
Initial saturation derives from provenance trust instead of a flat default.
Trust level is carried through governance and audit surfaces.

tasks.title: Add cross-model verification as pre-crystallization fiber pinning
tasks.description: Introduce multi-model verification as a stronger corroboration primitive so claims can be pinned or reheated before they harden into durable memory.
acceptance: Cross-model verification can strengthen or weaken crystallization candidates.
Verification outcomes map to explicit pinning or entropy events.
Unavailable verification degrades gracefully without forging confidence.

nestedPrioritySummary: P1: Build the long-term memory substrate after governance is trustworthy.
P2: Sequence temporal, reliability, and operator ergonomics after core provenance work.

phases.name: Cache Validation and Temporal Memory
phases.objective: Bind UOR identity, cache validation, decay profiles, and temporal chaining into one governed memory freshness model so stale or superseded memory fails closed instead of drifting forward.

tasks.title: Implement deterministic UOR cache validation contract
tasks.description: Validate cached documents, chunks, semantic nodes, and retrieval bundles against current UOR fingerprints so cache entries are treated as claims about graph state rather than trusted snapshots.
acceptance: Cache validation compares stored UOR fingerprints to current graph state.
Validation covers source documents, chunks, semantic nodes, and retrieval bundles.
Failed validation marks cache entries stale instead of trusting advisory timestamps.

tasks.title: Run cache validation in shadow mode and measure drift
tasks.description: Quantify invalidation rates, false positives, and migration gaps before cache validation becomes an enforcing gate on live retrieval paths.
acceptance: Shadow-mode validation produces measurable invalidation telemetry.
False positives and migration gaps are surfaced before fail-closed activation.
Shadow-mode results are written to a reviewable audit trail.

tasks.title: Promote cache validation to fail-closed enforcement
tasks.description: Block stale durable reuse, rehydrate invalid bundles from live state, and demote questionable summaries instead of letting stale memory propagate silently.
acceptance: Stale cache entries are blocked from durable reuse.
Invalid retrieval bundles are rehydrated from live graph state.
Failure paths are explicit and auditable rather than silent.

tasks.title: Assign temporal decay profiles and lazy retrieval weighting
tasks.description: Apply decay profiles at ingestion and use lazy temporal weighting during retrieval so memory can cool or fade without expensive global recomputation.
acceptance: Artifacts receive deterministic temporal profiles at ingestion.
Retrieval traces show when temporal weighting affects candidate rank.
Temporal weighting remains compatible with governance review.

tasks.title: Add temporal chaining and restaking for superseding memory
tasks.description: Create verifiable temporal lineage and a governed restaking path so superseded or operationally critical memories can be justified, revived, or downgraded without narrative handwaving.
acceptance: Superseding memories are linked through temporal chain metadata.
Critical decayed memories can be restaked with audit evidence.
Chain breaks or unjustified restakes surface as governance blockers.

nestedPrioritySummary: P1: Build the long-term memory substrate after governance is trustworthy.
P2: Sequence temporal, reliability, and operator ergonomics after core provenance work.

phases.name: Memory Reliability and Pressure Control
phases.objective: Give Victor a measurable reliability model so memory quality, pressure, and decay behavior are supervised by explicit SLOs instead of inferred from intuition.

tasks.title: Add memory SLO tracking and error budgets
tasks.description: Define recall, integrity, decay, and latency targets so Victor can detect when the memory system is degrading before bad memory reaches action selection.
acceptance: Memory SLOs are explicit and measurable.
Error budgets can be computed from current performance.
Reliability degradation can surface as an actionable governance signal.

tasks.title: Implement pressure-aware decay and adjusted half-life reporting
tasks.description: Compute memory pressure from tier utilization and expose the adjusted half-life so the system can quantify when pressure should accelerate forgetting.
acceptance: Pressure is computed from memory tier utilization.
Adjusted half-life is reported through the SLO surface.
Pressure-aware behavior remains a deterministic value function.

tasks.title: Add circuit breakers for exhausted reliability budgets
tasks.description: Pause crystallization, tighten decay, or force audit mode when reliability budgets are exhausted so degraded memory quality cannot silently self-authorize.
acceptance: Reliability failures can trigger explicit protective interventions.
Circuit-breaker actions are auditable and reversible.
Protective interventions integrate with existing memory governance policy.

nestedPrioritySummary: P1: Build the long-term memory substrate after governance is trustworthy.
P2: Sequence temporal, reliability, and operator ergonomics after core provenance work.

children.title: Extend artifact schema with epistemic type, governance state, provenance completeness

phases.name: Memory Operator Surface and Ergonomic API
phases.objective: Expose the completed memory system through usable operator and developer surfaces so the design is not trapped in low-level internals.

tasks.title: Add simplified facade for common memory operations
tasks.description: Wrap the full processor in an ergonomic API for add, search, feedback, dispute, and session control so the memory system is practical to consume without understanding every subsystem.
acceptance: A simplified facade supports the core memory workflow.
The facade preserves an escape hatch to the full processor.
Facade behavior remains aligned with governed memory semantics.

tasks.title: Add governed traversal and forgetting operations
tasks.description: Support association traversal and deliberate forgetting so the operator surface can inspect or remove memory with explicit graph semantics instead of brittle data surgery.
acceptance: Operators can inspect related memory through graph traversal.
Forgetting removes memory cleanly from governed tiers and edges.
Delete and traversal actions are visible in audit output.

tasks.title: Add file and document ingestion surface
tasks.description: Expose file and document ingestion so external materials can enter the governed memory pipeline with preserved provenance instead of bypassing the memory system.
acceptance: File ingestion supports the intended document classes.
Chunking, encoding, and routing preserve source provenance.
Ingestion remains policy-checked instead of bypassing governance.

tasks.title: Add operator views for chain, decay, and governance inspection
tasks.description: Expose the memory ledger, chain state, decay status, and governance decisions through operator-facing views so Victor's memory system is legible during review and debugging.
acceptance: Operators can inspect chain state, decay state, and governance state.
Views distinguish grounded memory evidence from inferred summaries.
Inspection surfaces support review without requiring code-level tracing.

nestedPrioritySummary: P1: Build the long-term memory substrate after governance is trustworthy.
P2: Sequence temporal, reliability, and operator ergonomics after core provenance work.

phases.name: EvolveAI Recursive Learning and Model Governance
phases.objective: Add the missing EvolveAI track so recursive learning, model governance, and self-improvement proposals become explicit product work instead of ambient ambition.
nestedPrioritySummary: P0: Learning packets and self-improvement proposals are governed artifacts, not informal notes.
P1: EvolveAI state is visible in Builder alongside memory and execution state.
P1: Model/runtime changes require explicit review and rollback posture.

tasks.title: Formalize EvolveAI learning packets and improvement proposals
tasks.description: Represent self-improvement opportunities as governed packets with evidence, risk classification, expected benefit, rollback conditions, and promotion status.
acceptance: Learning packets have a durable schema with evidence and rollback fields.
Improvement proposals are distinct from observed lessons and failures.
Packets can be reviewed, accepted, rejected, or quarantined explicitly.

children.title: Define packet schema for lesson, proposal, experiment, rollback
Bind packets to evidence in memory and execution ledgers

tasks.title: Expose EvolveAI state in Forge UI
tasks.description: Show learning queues, proposals, active experiments, and blocked self-modification items in the same operator surface as roadmap and memory state.
acceptance: Builder shows EvolveAI queues and proposal state.
Operators can see why proposals are blocked or pending.
EvolveAI visualization links back to roadmap phases and evidence.

children.title: Add visual hooks for proposal queue, experiment state, blocked changes
Trace each item back to roadmap and evidence artifacts

tasks.title: Gate model/runtime self-modification through explicit governance
tasks.description: Require review, rollback planning, and bounded experiment definitions before EvolveAI can alter model selection, prompts, adapters, or runtime behavior.
acceptance: Self-modification proposals require explicit review and rollback posture.
Changes are bounded by experiment definitions before activation.
Model/runtime governance stays legible to the operator.

children.title: Require rollback strategy and blast-radius declaration
Separate low-risk tuning from structural runtime changes

tasks.title: Close the loop from runtime outcomes back into EvolveAI
tasks.description: Ensure wins, failures, and contradictions from execution and memory governance feed the recursive learning queue instead of disappearing into narrative residue.
acceptance: Execution and memory outcomes can generate EvolveAI intake items.
Contradictions and failures produce feedback that survives beyond a single session.
Learning loops remain auditable and disableable.

children.title: Ingest execution outcomes into EvolveAI intake
Turn contradiction and failure events into queued learning work

phases.name: Evidence Layer API Wiring
phases.objective: Expose evidence-layer contract (evaluate, log, bundle) through Continuum API routes and wire the write-side so governance lifecycle events automatically produce evidence records.

tasks.title: Create evidence API route handlers on Continuum service
tasks.description: Add POST /api/continuum/evidence/evaluate, /evidence/log, /evidence/bundle endpoints that call the existing contract.ts, evaluate.ts, log.ts, bundle.ts modules.

tasks.title: Wire governance lifecycle events to evidence logger
tasks.description: Hook plan, audit, implement, substantiate events into the evidence append-only ledger so every lifecycle transition is recorded automatically.

tasks.title: Add evidence proxy paths to zo.space API route
tasks.description: Extend the /api/continuum/graph proxy ALLOWED list with evidence endpoints.

tasks.title: Write integration tests for evidence API endpoints
tasks.description: Test evaluate, log, and bundle endpoints return correct shapes and persist to the evidence ledger.

phases.name: Evidence Recording in Governance Lifecycle
phases.objective: Ensure every governance lifecycle event (plan creation, audit verdict, implementation seal, substantiation) automatically produces a structured evidence record linked to Continuum.

tasks.title: Define evidence record schema for each lifecycle event type
tasks.description: Specify the shape of evidence records for plan, audit, implement, and substantiate events with provenance hashes.

tasks.title: Instrument qor-plan skill to emit evidence on plan creation
tasks.description: After a plan is created and committed, emit an evidence record to the evidence ledger.

tasks.title: Instrument qor-audit skill to emit evidence on verdict
tasks.description: After audit PASS/VETO, emit evidence with the verdict, chain hash, and flagged items.

tasks.title: Instrument qor-substantiate to emit evidence on seal
tasks.description: After Merkle seal, emit a final evidence record linking all prior evidence for the session.

phases.name: Victor Audit Page Rewire
phases.objective: Rewire the /qor/victor zo.space page to consume real promotion-gate, soak-evidence, and heartbeat data from the Victor kernel APIs instead of stubs.

tasks.title: Expose promotion-gate summary as a Victor API endpoint
tasks.description: Create GET /api/victor/promotion-gate that returns the PromotionGateSummary from promotion-gate.ts.

tasks.title: Expose soak-evidence summary as a Victor API endpoint
tasks.description: Create GET /api/victor/soak-evidence that returns the SoakEvidenceSummary.

tasks.title: Update /qor/victor page to fetch and display real promotion data
tasks.description: Replace stubbed promotion status with live API calls to promotion-gate and soak-evidence endpoints.

tasks.title: Write tests for Victor API endpoints
tasks.description: Test promotion-gate and soak-evidence endpoints return correct shapes.

phases.name: Continuum Ingestion Reliability
phases.objective: Harden the Continuum ingestion pipeline to handle edge cases: duplicate records, malformed JSONL, missing embeddings, and stale heartbeat files.

tasks.title: Add deduplication guard to memory-to-graph ingest
tasks.description: Check record fingerprint before inserting to Neo4j, skip duplicates.

tasks.title: Add JSONL validation before ingestion
tasks.description: Validate each line parses as valid JSON with required fields before graph write.

tasks.title: Handle missing embeddings gracefully in batch clustering
tasks.description: Skip records without embeddings in cluster pipeline instead of erroring (audit flag F2 hardening).

tasks.title: Write tests for ingestion edge cases
tasks.description: Test duplicate rejection, malformed JSONL handling, and missing embedding graceful degradation.

phases.name: Dead Service and Route Cleanup
phases.objective: Identify and remove dead zo.space routes, unused API endpoints, and orphaned service registrations that no longer serve live functionality.

tasks.title: Audit all zo.space routes for liveness
tasks.description: List all routes, test each for 200/404, flag dead ones.

tasks.title: Audit registered user services for liveness
tasks.description: List all registered services, check if their ports respond, flag dead ones.

tasks.title: Remove confirmed dead routes and services
tasks.description: Delete routes and unregister services confirmed dead after audit.

tasks.title: Document active service inventory
tasks.description: Update SYSTEM_STATE with the canonical list of active services, ports, and routes.

phases.name: Governance Memory Integration
phases.objective: Record all governance decisions (plan verdicts, audit results, VETO patterns, substantiation seals) as first-class Continuum episodic records so the intelligence layers can derive governance patterns.

tasks.title: Define governance episodic record types
tasks.description: Extend EpisodicRecord with governance-specific fields: verdict, chain_hash, session_id, lifecycle_stage.

tasks.title: Write governance events to Neo4j on lifecycle transitions
tasks.description: Hook into META_LEDGER append to also write governance nodes to the graph.

tasks.title: Enable semantic derivation over governance records
tasks.description: Ensure co-occurrence and clustering include governance records, surfacing recurring decision patterns.

tasks.title: Write tests for governance record ingestion
tasks.description: Test that plan, audit, implement, and seal events create proper graph nodes.

phases.name: Shadow Genome Graph Projection
phases.objective: Project SHADOW_GENOME failure patterns and mandatory guards into Neo4j as queryable graph nodes, enabling the procedural layer to mine anti-patterns.

tasks.title: Parse SHADOW_GENOME.md into structured records
tasks.description: Extract VETO patterns, mandatory guards, and failure modes from the Shadow Genome document.

tasks.title: Create Shadow Genome node types in Neo4j
tasks.description: Define :ShadowGenome:Pattern and :ShadowGenome:Guard labels with provenance edges.

tasks.title: Wire procedural mining to check against shadow patterns
tasks.description: When mining procedures, cross-reference against shadow genome to flag anti-patterns.

tasks.title: Write tests for shadow genome graph operations
tasks.description: Test parsing, graph insertion, and anti-pattern detection.

phases.name: Workcell Formal State Machine
phases.objective: Replace ad-hoc task status transitions with a formal state machine that enforces valid lifecycle transitions: planned → active → blocked/done, with governance gates at each transition.

tasks.title: Define the workcell state machine transitions
tasks.description: Specify valid state transitions with guards: planned→active requires plan, active→done requires substantiation.

tasks.title: Implement state machine enforcement in task updates
tasks.description: Reject invalid transitions in updateTaskStatus with clear error messages.

tasks.title: Add governance gate checks at transition boundaries
tasks.description: Before transitioning to 'done', verify evidence exists. Before 'active', verify plan exists.

tasks.title: Write tests for state machine enforcement
tasks.description: Test all valid transitions succeed and invalid transitions are rejected.

phases.name: Runtime Trust Stage Enforcement
phases.objective: Enforce the 5-tier trust progression at runtime so Victor cannot escalate beyond its earned autonomy level, with automatic demotion on governance violations.

tasks.title: Add trust stage validation to heartbeat execution
tasks.description: Before executing any action, verify the current trust tier permits it.

tasks.title: Implement automatic demotion on governance violation
tasks.description: When a governance denial or contradiction is detected, drop trust tier by one level.

tasks.title: Add trust stage audit trail to heartbeat ledger
tasks.description: Log every trust stage transition (promotion/demotion) with reason and evidence.

tasks.title: Write tests for trust enforcement and demotion
tasks.description: Test that violations trigger demotion and tier checks prevent unauthorized actions.

phases.name: Autonomy Boundary Runtime Limits
phases.objective: Define and enforce runtime resource limits per autonomy level: action budgets, time windows, file access scope, and API call limits that scale with trust tier.

tasks.title: Define resource limits per autonomy level
tasks.description: Specify max actions/tick, max files modified, max API calls, and time window per tier.

tasks.title: Implement resource limit enforcement in heartbeat
tasks.description: Track resource consumption per tick and enforce limits, blocking when exceeded.

tasks.title: Add resource consumption metrics to heartbeat state
tasks.description: Expose current consumption vs limits in the heartbeat state snapshot.

tasks.title: Write tests for resource limit enforcement
tasks.description: Test that exceeding limits blocks further actions and consumption tracking is accurate.
